title: Photographic Process Workers and Processing Machine Operators
description: Perform work involved in developing and processing photographic images from film or digital media. May perform precision tasks such as editing photographic negatives and prints.

tasks.text: Select digital images for printing, specify number of images to be printed, and direct to printer, using computer software.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Create prints according to customer specifications and laboratory protocols.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Produce color or black-and-white photographs, negatives, or slides, applying standard photographic reproduction techniques and procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set or adjust machine controls, according to specifications, type of operation, or material requirements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review computer-processed digital images for quality.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Operate scanners or related computer equipment to digitize negatives, photographic prints, or other images.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fill tanks of processing machines with solutions such as developer, dyes, stop-baths, fixers, bleaches, or washes.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Measure and mix chemicals to prepare solutions for processing, according to formulas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Load digital images onto computers directly from cameras or from storage devices, such as flash memory cards or universal serial bus (USB) devices.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Operate special equipment to perform tasks such as transferring film to videotape or producing photographic enlargements.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine developed prints for defects, such as broken lines, spots, or blurs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Read work orders to determine required processes, techniques, materials, or equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Load circuit boards, racks or rolls of film, negatives, or printing paper into processing or printing machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Insert processed negatives and prints into envelopes for delivery to customers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Reprint originals for enlargement or in sections to be pieced together.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean or maintain photoprocessing or darkroom equipment, using ultrasonic equipment or cleaning and rinsing solutions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor equipment operation to detect malfunctions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Maintain records, such as quantities or types of processing completed, materials used, or customer charges.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Immerse film, negatives, paper, or prints in developing solutions, fixing solutions, and water to complete photographic development processes.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine quality of film fades or dissolves for potential color corrections, using color analyzers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Thread filmstrips through densitometers or sensitometers and expose film to light to determine density of film, necessary color corrections, or light sensitivity.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine drawings, negatives, or photographic prints to determine coloring, shading, accenting, or other changes required for retouching or restoration.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place sensitized paper in frames of projection printers, photostats, or other reproduction machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Upload digital images onto Web sites for customers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Produce timed prints with separate densities or color settings for each scene of a production.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Splice broken or separated film and mount film on reels.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Retouch photographic negatives or original prints to correct defects.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
